page: Microsoft Azure F2ams_v6 is a memory-intensive virtual machine belonging to the Famsv6 server family. It is powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz. The instance features 2 dedicated physical cores with 2 vCPUs at 1.0 thread per core, and it is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, though it is premium storage capable.

The underlying AMD EPYC processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 (AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16) and cryptographic acceleration via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI. In benchmark testing, the single-core performance is highly competitive, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranking in the top tier (top 10%) and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core scaling is limited by the dual-core configuration, resulting in average to weak multi-core benchmark results, such as in Geekbench multi-core and PassMark CPU tests.

Memory bandwidth benchmarks show mixed results, with cached small-block reads and uncached large-block reads performing in the bottom 25%, while large-block write bandwidth remains average. The instance lacks local NVMe storage and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and strong single-threaded performance, the F2ams_v6 is suited for memory-intensive workloads that do not require massive parallelization, such as lightweight database operations, text processing, and small-scale language model inference.
description: Microsoft Azure F2ams_v6 is a memory-intensive virtual machine from the Famsv6 family, built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. Operating at 3.7 GHz, it provides 2 dedicated physical cores (2 vCPUs) paired with 16.0 GB of RAM, establishing an 8.0 GB per core memory density. The instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs but supports premium storage. Performance benchmarks highlight strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core results in the top 10% and strong single-threaded text processing. However, multi-core performance and memory read bandwidth are relatively weak due to the dual-core constraint. This virtual machine is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-heavy, low-concurrency tasks, making it suitable for lightweight database operations, text processing, and small-scale LLM inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F2ams_v6 is a memory-intensive virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and strong single-core performance for low-concurrency workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F2ams_v6 is a memory-intensive VM with 2 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16.0 GB RAM, and top-tier single-core CPU performance.
tagline: A memory-intensive dual-core virtual machine delivering strong single-threaded performance and high memory density for low-concurrency workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz
Configured with 16.0 GB of RAM and 2 dedicated vCPUs
Top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
No bundled local storage or integrated GPUs
Best suited for memory-intensive, low-concurrency workloads and text processing
